title: ageratinastrum palustre
intro: Ageratinastrum palustre, also known as the marsh Ageratina or water shrub, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ageratinastrum palustre include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and treat various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and stems, and can be administered as teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
